reasoning: Stock returns over short periods are fundamentally noisy, and predictions should stay close to 0.5. MKTX experienced a sharp 5-day drop from ~$173 to $161 just prior to the forecast date. The `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool offered a short-term probability of 0.58 for the price to exceed $161 based on seasonal patterns compensating for negative linear momentum. I have adjusted this closer to base-rate levels as the seasonality signal is based on minimal historical data (1 year), setting the immediate-term forecast (Nov 30) at 54%. The recent upgrade from Zacks ("strong sell" to "hold") following the dip further supports stabilization. As instructed, I have pulled the estimates for the more distant resolution dates (Dec 23, Feb 21) progressively closer to 0.5, representing the fading of the short-term signal and an increase in broader uncertainty over longer horizons.
